title: deprecation-queue.yaml — baseline of file-level deprecation markers and remaining importers
description: Machine-readable spec of the .dw-lifecycle/scope-discovery/deprecation-queue.yaml wire format. The deprecation scanner (`dw-lifecycle check-deprecations`) discovers entries by walking the source tree for file-level `@deprecated` JSDoc tags and `// DEPRECATED:` line comments — the YAML is NOT the source of truth for which files are deprecated (the markers in source are). The file is seeded empty by `install-scope-discovery` and may be populated by a future enhancement that persists scan baselines (mirroring `clones.yaml`'s role for the clone detector). v1 readers tolerate an empty file or an empty `deprecations: []` array. Runtime behavior: the scanner ignores this file; `--write` emits the rendered markdown to `deprecation-queue.md` (NOT this YAML). The schema is provided for editor autocomplete + Phase 9 doctor rule symmetry with the sibling registries.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Seeded as 1; bump on backward-incompatible changes to the shape. The seed always sets this so the doctor's schema-stale rule has a known-good baseline.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent scan that produced this baseline. Optional in v1 (the v1 scanner does not write this file); reserved for a future enhancement that persists scan results.

deprecations.description: All deprecated files surfaced by the most recent scan. Empty list is the seeded state and is valid (the scanner discovers markers in source; the YAML is informational). Order is not significant; paths must be unique.

DeprecationEntry.description: One deprecated source file. `path` is repo-relative POSIX. `marker_kind` discriminates the two v1 detection paths (top-of-file JSDoc `@deprecated` vs. `// DEPRECATED:` line comment within the first 20 lines). `message` is the trimmed text after the marker (empty string when the marker carries no inline message). `importers` is the resolved set of external importers; each importer is a repo-relative POSIX path + 1-based line number of the first matching import statement.

path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path of the deprecated file.

marker_kind.description: Detection path. `jsdoc` = top-of-file JSDoc `@deprecated` tag; `inline` = `// DEPRECATED:` line comment within the first 20 lines.

message.description: Trimmed message text after the marker. Empty string when the marker carries no inline content (e.g., a bare `@deprecated` tag with no following prose).

importers.description: External importers of the deprecated file. Each entry is a repo-relative POSIX path + 1-based line number of the first import statement matching the deprecated file's `@/` alias OR its basename via a relative path. The deprecated file's own contents are NOT included (internal re-exports don't count as external importers). Empty list means the file is safe to delete.

status.description: Phase 11 Task 2 — Loop status. See plugins/dw-lifecycle/src/scope-discovery/util/catalog-status.ts. Reserved for future use (v1 deprecation scanner discovers entries from source markers; the YAML is a placeholder for future persistence).

provenance.description: Phase 11 Task 2 — provenance block. Reserved for future use.

audit_history.description: Phase 11 Task 10 — REVERSE provenance link to the audit-log. Reserved for future use.

Importer.description: One external importer of a deprecated file.

path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path of the importer.

line.description: 1-based line number of the first matching import statement in the importer file.
